tracks.title: Electric Color
tracks.loreNote: The solid #2 hit. It sat at the runner-up spot for six weeks, famously blocked from #1 by a novelty song.

tracks.title: Plastic Sky
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's first act of 'malicious compliance.' Anti-corporate lyrics hidden inside a polished, studio-approved pop-rock wrapper.

tracks.title: My Anchor Holds
tracks.loreNote: A power ballad the studio marketed as a romance, but which the band actually wrote about their unbreakable family bond.

tracks.title: Dancing in the Neon
tracks.loreNote: A safe, polished synth-pop single pushed heavily by Apex Records, prioritizing high energy over the band's rock roots.

tracks.title: Running on the Edge
tracks.loreNote: A protest song featuring angsty, anti-corporate lyrics hidden inside a 'safe' pop-rock wrapper.

tracks.title: Just a Girl
tracks.loreNote: Cassidy's quiet rebellion. Dismissed by executives as filler, it became a massive grassroots anthem for female fans.

tracks.title: Light Up the Night
tracks.loreNote: The song Apex poured all their marketing money into. It was meant to be the 'Big One' but completely failed to connect.

tracks.title: Break the Walls
tracks.loreNote: The one 'pure rock' anthem Apex allowed on the album. It became an immediate fan-favorite for its raw energy.

tracks.title: Worlds Apart
tracks.loreNote: A soaring duet that served as meta-commentary on the studio's attempt to separate their 'Stardust' and 'Engine' personas.

tracks.title: Another Night
tracks.loreNote: A safe filler track demanded by Apex to pad the runtime. Ryan was explicitly banned from playing guitar on it.

tracks.title: Out of Time
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's punk-inspired vent. Apex gleefully cut this raw track from the original vinyl release to save space.

tracks.title: Rearview Mirror
tracks.loreNote: A moody synth-pop track Cassidy used to express her feeling of being a suppressed 'passenger' in her own career.

tracks.title: The Stardust Engine
tracks.loreNote: Buried at the album's end, this true anthem perfectly blended synth and rock. It could have spent 11 weeks at #1.
